events.label: Devin Jatho + 329k followers (verified tick)

events.text: My name is Devon Jato and my clients name is anywhere from $300,000 to $500,000 a year

events.label: Average Revenue per client (empty)
$337,720 (green) + exponential line chart begins
$507,113 final, full green curve

events.text: to write viral social media scripts for them.

events.label: cut to three-thumbnail social-proof stack
2M / 3.2M / 1.7M view-count thumbnails

events.text: And these are the two websites that I use across every single viral script that I make.

events.label: VidYard (green alien) + Hemingway (H|) over blurred Google Doc background
1-frame full-frame RGB split + scanline ghost as emphasis-caption reveal

events.font_family_guess: Obviously Wide Black / Proxima Extra Condensed Black / Druk Wide

events.text: Now my goal is to keep every single script as short and simple as possible.

events.label: cut to full-screen Google Doc window, subject at ~40% opacity behind
SHORT (scissors) + SIMPLE (star) slide from left, ~150ms stagger

events.text: So the first website that I use to keep in the script as short as possible is going to be a video art script timer.

events.label: full-screen VidYard icon centered, subject A-roll behind, subtle bob
vidyard.com/script-timer/ typed character-by-character with blinking cursor
4 ffmpeg scene-detection hits at 21.17/21.21/21.25/21.29 are character-reveal frames, NOT real cuts

events.text: Now this website will tell you exactly on how long it will take for you to read out your script.

events.label: full-screen Google Doc with VidYard '59 seconds' popup overlaid
zoom into Google Doc CTA section, above content fades to 20% opacity
over-the-shoulder of real dual-monitor setup, LED lamp, purple room, timer shows '0 seconds'
authentic OTS B-roll with shallow DOF, purple LED ambient, moon lamp practical
0.04s flash between doc states during timer update (handle-divider animation)
timer: 56s -> 53s -> 46s, syncs to narration of 'lower by 10 seconds'

events.text: Then once I'm knocked it down by at least 10 seconds, I go ahead and copy that script to show you over to the second website, himmingwayapp.com.

events.label: A-roll, Hemingway H| icon composited over subject's chest, subtle zoom-bob
A-roll with 'hemingway|' URL pill typing animation
custom dark navy Hemingway UI with metrics cards, 'Readability: Grade 9 Good.' label
Hemingway 'hard to read' phrase boxes animate in over the script

events.text: Now this one results will tell you the reading level of your script and then highlight which part of your script might be too complex for your viewer.

events.label: same 1-frame RGB glitch as 10.38, again as caption reveal

events.text: A good rule of thumb that I follow is that the great level of your script has to be a grade 7 reading level or below

events.label: italic 'Readability' label + 10 colored dots + white triangle pointer at position 6
pointer slides 6 -> 7, dots 8/9/10 fade out

events.text: The average reading level in the U.S. is generally around the 7th-grade level. However, a significant portion of the adult population reads below this level, with many struggling with even basic literacy tasks.
mainly because that is the average reading level in the United States.

events.label: A-roll with VidYard + Hemingway icons faded to 30% behind three '???' boxes

events.text: These are two out of the five of my favorite scripting websites.

events.label: B-roll composite: framed reference-video inset + 3 '???' boxes, blurred duplicate as background
inner-clip content swaps to Microsoft Word 'Viral Script' doc
LESS TIME (timer icon) + LESS EFFORT (wrench icon) slide from left
A-roll, subject making double peace/V-sign; iMessage-style comment pill 'Lin' with up-arrow send button
dark rounded card with IG logo, 'Devin Jatho' / 'now' / 'Here you go handsome man' simulating DM reply

events.text: Now I just posted two video breaking down all of the tools I've used while scripting. So if you want to send that over to you along with the two websites that I mentioned in this video, then go and comment the word link and I'll send it to you.

signature_elements: emphasis captions reserved for narrative turns only (not wall-to-wall captions)
1-frame RGB glitch transition for caption reveals
purple LED rim light (blue only in hook)
comment-gate CTA with simulated IG DM reveal
style_summary.caption_philosophy: sparse emphasis captions (2 in 69s) — NOT auto-transcribed subtitles. Replace any instinct to auto-caption with UI-style graphic overlays.
